title: Backward Induction in a Sequential Game
description: A flowchart demonstrating the backward induction process used to find a subgame perfect Nash equilibrium in a sequential game by solving from the final decision nodes backwards to the root.
elements: Payoff Nodes (Terminal nodes with utility values)
relationships: Process flows from terminal payoffs backwards to the root, at each node selecting the action that maximizes the payoff for the player whose turn it is, given the future rational play of opponents.
